title: 36" x 15" x 24" Above-Fridge Wall Cabinet
summary: A shallow wall cabinet sized to bridge the gap above a refrigerator, hung from an internal support rather than a face-frame ledge.
description:
A cabinet mounted this high carries no weight from below, so every bit of its strength has to come from how it hangs — that's what the inset back-top support is for. Get that support piece solidly pocket-screwed to the sides and back before you trust anything on the shelves above it, and add a second support strip at the base if you plan to store anything heavier than lightweight boxes up there.

Unlike the base cabinets in the same kitchen system, the back panel here is genuinely non-structural — it just closes in the interior — which is why it's fine to attach with simple finish nails and glue instead of pocket screws. The face frame follows the same reveal logic as the rest of the run: it overhangs the sides slightly so neighboring cabinets and end panels line up flush across the whole kitchen.

tools.note: For ripping the plywood sides and top/bottom to width.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Sides and top/bottom, ripped to 22-1/4" wide.

materials.name: Pine, 1x4, 4 ft
materials.note: Back-top hanging support — plywood scrap also works.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes and build the carcass
steps.body: On the outsides of the sides and top/bottom pieces, drill 3/4" pocket holes along the front edges — these will be covered by neighboring cabinets or, on an end cabinet, by matching finished plywood. Then, working from the outsides, drill 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the top and bottom and attach to the sides with 1-1/4" pocket screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the hanging support
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the top edge and ends of the back-top support piece and attach it inset at the back of the carcass — this is what the cabinet actually hangs from, so make sure it's solidly fastened to both sides. Add a second support strip at the base if you're planning to store anything heavier than light boxes.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: The back is not structural here — it only closes in the cabinet's interior — so attach it with 3/4" finish nails and glue rather than pocket screws.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame as a separate assembly on the bench, then attach the completed frame to the front of the cabinet.

steps.title: Hang the doors
steps.body: Install the two full-overlay doors with concealed overlay hinges sized for a 1-1/4" overlay.

images.alt: Shallow wall kitchen cabinet sized to fit above a refrigerator, with a face frame and two doors
